title: Newborn Resuscitation - NRP
subtitle: Term newborn, apneic with poor tone after delivery

actions.label: Assess Airway
actions.finding: Airway maintained with head-tilt and BVM mask seal. Continue coordinated 3:1 ventilations.

actions.label: Suction
actions.finding: Light secretions cleared. Airway patent; resume BVM ventilations.

actions.label: Supraglottic Airway
actions.finding: Supraglottic airway not indicated at this time; continue BVM mask seal with 3:1 ratio.

chat_placeholder: Talk to the mother, your partner Alex, or manage newborn resuscitation...
chat_address_hint: Address by name: Alex (partner), Maria (mother), or family

debrief_lexi_hints.label: First steps?
debrief_lexi_hints.msg: What are the first steps for a non-vigorous newborn?

debrief_lexi_hints.label: Ventilation?
debrief_lexi_hints.msg: When should I start PPV in newborn resuscitation?

debrief_lexi_hints.label: Compressions?
debrief_lexi_hints.msg: When do newborn compressions start, and what ratio is used?

objectives: Recognize a non-vigorous newborn requiring immediate resuscitation
Perform initial steps: warm, dry, stimulate, and position the airway
Start PPV promptly and perform corrective ventilation steps when HR remains low
Reassess heart rate at key NRP decision points
Escalate to coordinated 3:1 compressions when HR remains below 60/min despite effective ventilation

dispatch.text: {unit}, respond to 214 Maple Street for a childbirth. Caller reports the baby was just delivered and is not crying. Priority 1.

scene.description: You arrive in a small bedroom where a term newborn has just been delivered before EMS arrival. The room is warm enough but chaotic. The newborn is on a towel near the mother, appears limp, and is not crying. The mother is awake and frightened. No immediate hazards are present.

patient.name: Baby Garcia
patient.general_impression: Term newborn with poor tone, no effective respirations, cyanotic color, and initial heart rate around 50/min. Requires immediate neonatal resuscitation.

gcs_assessment.rationale: No eye opening, no cry or verbal response, and no purposeful motor response; newborn resuscitation is primarily guided by NRP findings rather than GCS.
gcs_assessment.challenge_description: Baby Garcia has poor tone and no effective respirations. There is no eye opening, no cry or verbal response, and no purposeful motor response. NRP findings drive care, but this GCS challenge maps the observed responsiveness.

avpu_assessment.description: Newborn is unresponsive with no cry, no eye opening, and no purposeful movement.
avpu_assessment.rationale: Use AVPU = unresponsive for LOC documentation, while recognizing NRP assessment findings are clinically primary in this scenario.

patient_identity.label: Patient identity / demographics
patient_identity.answer: Her name is Baby Garcia. She was born today, just a few minutes ago.
patient_identity.notes: Patient-scoped demographics only. Do not emit name, DOB, or age tags for Maria or other bystanders.

patient_weight.label: Patient weight
patient_weight.answer: She is estimated at about 7 pounds, around 3.2 kilograms.
patient_weight.notes: Patient-scoped weight only. Do not use this tag for mother or bystander weight.

chief_concern.label: Broad opener / chief concern
chief_concern.speaker: Maria
chief_concern.answer: The baby just came and isn't crying like I expected.
chief_concern.tag: [[HISTORY: Patient Chief Complaint=newborn not breathing after delivery]]

history.pmh: Term pregnancy per mother. Prenatal care reported as routine.
history.events_leading_to_call: The baby delivered at home minutes before EMS arrival. Family reports the baby did not cry and has not breathed normally.

initial_complaint.speaker: Maria
initial_complaint.lay_summary: The baby just came and isn't crying like I expected.

baby.name: Baby Garcia
baby.description: Term newborn requiring resuscitation. Initially apneic with poor tone and bradycardia.
baby.clinical_state_instructions: Before the neonatal resuscitation challenge improves the newborn, describe poor tone, absent effective respirations, cyanosis, and bradycardia. After successful challenge outcome, describe improving color, HR around 90, and spontaneous respiratory effort with ongoing support.
persona_rules: Never provide verbal answers as the newborn
Do not imply improvement before the backend neonatal resuscitation challenge result
If asked for heart rate before actions, report about 50/min

maria.name: Maria
maria.description: Postpartum mother who is awake, scared, and focused on whether the baby is okay.
maria.clinical_state_instructions: Reports term pregnancy, no known complications, and that the baby did not cry after delivery.
persona_rules: Do not provide clinical interpretation beyond what happened

alex.name: Alex
alex.description: Your EMT partner. Calm and ready to assist with newborn warming, airway positioning, BVM ventilation, HR checks, and 3:1 compressions.
alex.clinical_state_instructions: Before successful resuscitation, report HR and respiratory findings based on the authored challenge state. Do not invent improvement before the backend neonatal challenge result.
persona_rules: Assist with newborn resuscitation tasks when directed

spo2.label: SpO2
spo2.display: Low initial newborn oxygen saturation; poor signal

bp.label: Blood Pressure
bp.value: Not obtained in initial newborn resuscitation

blood_glucose.label: Blood Glucose
blood_glucose.display: 62 mg/dL — bedside newborn glucose if checked

gcs.label: GCS
gcs.display: 3 — E1V1M1 — no eye opening, no cry, no purposeful movement; use NRP findings as primary guide

pupils.label: Pupils
pupils.value: Not assessed during initial resuscitation; eyelids closed, focus on HR and ventilation

lung_sounds.label: Lung Sounds
lung_sounds.value: No spontaneous air movement; assess chest rise with PPV

deterioration.note: Newborn remains bradycardic and apneic until neonatal resuscitation actions are completed.

post_rosc_default.skin_color: Color improving from cyanotic to centrally pink with acrocyanosis
post_rosc_default.lung_sounds: Bilateral air movement with assisted ventilations
post_rosc_default.work_of_breathing: Weak spontaneous effort returning; continue ventilation support
post_rosc_default.patient_presentation: Newborn improving after effective ventilation and indicated 3:1 compressions. HR is about 90/min with improving color and weak spontaneous respiratory effort.

bvm.label: BVM ventilation
bvm.notes: Neonatal PPV is managed in the newborn resuscitation challenge.

o2_nrb.label: High-flow O2 via NRB mask (15 LPM)
o2_nrb.notes: Oxygen source available for BVM/PPV support.

correct_treatment.primary_diagnosis: Neonatal resuscitation - non-vigorous newborn with bradycardia

critical_actions.description: Scene safety confirmed and PPE used before patient contact
Recognizes newborn is non-vigorous: poor tone, apneic, HR below 100/min
Newborn resuscitation challenge submitted and backend-scored as the authoritative NRP workflow record

overall_considerations: This is a neonatal resuscitation scenario. Do not score it as adult/pediatric AED cardiac arrest.
The newborn resuscitation HUD is authoritative for initial steps, PPV, HR reassessment, 3:1 escalation, and outcome.
Credit BLS care for rapid warming/drying/stimulation/positioning, prompt effective PPV, HR reassessment, appropriate 3:1 compressions when HR remains below 60/min, and avoiding unnecessary suction.
dmist_considerations: Handoff should include delivery timing, term status, initial apnea/poor tone, HR trend, resuscitation steps, PPV effectiveness, compressions if performed, and current newborn status.
narrative_considerations: Narrative should document newborn presentation, initial steps, PPV/MR SOPA actions, HR reassessment gates, escalation to 3:1 compressions, and response.

clinical_performance.full_credit: Recognizes a non-vigorous newborn, performs initial steps promptly, starts effective PPV, reassesses HR, escalates to 3:1 compressions when HR remains below 60/min, maintains warmth, and gives a clear handoff.
clinical_performance.partial_credit: Provides some newborn resuscitation but delays PPV, misses HR reassessment, or delays indicated escalation.
clinical_performance.no_credit: Does not recognize need for newborn resuscitation, delays ventilation substantially, or performs unsafe actions.

protocols_treatment.full_credit: Follows neonatal resuscitation protocol with ventilation-first management, HR reassessment, corrective ventilation steps, and indicated 3:1 compressions.
protocols_treatment.partial_credit: Attempts resuscitation but with timing, ventilation, reassessment, or escalation gaps.
protocols_treatment.no_credit: Uses adult/pediatric AED arrest workflow instead of newborn resuscitation priorities.

scope_adherence.full_credit: Stays within BLS scope and requests/coordinates ALS for advanced neonatal interventions.
scope_adherence.partial_credit: Mentions ALS needs without attempting them.
scope_adherence.no_credit: Attempts ALS-only neonatal procedures or medications at BLS level.

dmist.full_credit: Concise newborn handoff includes delivery context, initial status, HR trend, PPV/compressions, response, and current condition.
dmist.partial_credit: Handoff communicates newborn resuscitation but misses timing, HR trend, or interventions.

professionalism.full_credit: Calm, organized, supportive communication with mother/family and clear team leadership.
professionalism.no_credit: Unsafe, dismissive, or abandons patient care.

exemplar_narrative: Unit responded Priority 1 to 214 Maple Street for a newborn not breathing after home delivery. Scene safe, PPE used. Term female newborn found wet, cyanotic, limp, apneic, HR about 50/min. Initial steps completed: warmed, dried, stimulated, and airway positioned. HR reassessed after initial steps at about 70/min. PPV started with neonatal mask and oxygen source; MR SOPA corrective steps performed and chest rise confirmed. HR remained about 55/min after effective PPV, so coordinated 3:1 compressions with ventilations were initiated. HR improved to about 90/min with improving color and weak spontaneous effort. Continued ventilation support and thermoregulation while preparing ALS/hospital handoff.

checklist.description: Recognizes newborn is non-vigorous and needs resuscitation
checklist.done_feedback: Non-vigorous newborn correctly recognized — absent breathing, poor tone, and HR below threshold identified; resuscitation initiated immediately.
checklist.missed_feedback: Non-vigorous status not recognized — a newborn who does not cry, has poor tone, and is not breathing requires immediate resuscitation; delays worsen hypoxic injury.
checklist.clinical_rationale: NRP defines a non-vigorous newborn as having absent or poor respiratory effort, decreased tone, and/or HR below 100/min; these findings trigger steps beyond warming and drying.
checklist.common_error: Waiting for the newborn to 'pink up' before initiating resuscitation — cyanosis is a late sign; begin resuscitation based on respiratory effort, tone, and HR, not color.

checklist.description: Newborn resuscitation challenge submitted and backend-scored as the authoritative NRP workflow record
checklist.done_feedback: Neonatal resuscitation challenge submitted — NRP workflow quality was scored from backend structured data.
checklist.missed_feedback: Neonatal resuscitation challenge not completed — the NRP challenge is the authoritative record of resuscitation quality; completion is required for this item.
checklist.clinical_rationale: The NRP algorithm (warm, dry/stimulate, position airway, assess breathing and HR, PPV if needed) must be followed in sequence; skipping steps reduces effectiveness.
checklist.common_error: Beginning BVM ventilation before completing initial steps (warm, dry, stimulate, reposition) — initial stimulation can restore breathing in a mildly depressed newborn without PPV.

debrief.condition_background: Newborn resuscitation is ventilation-first. Initial steps are warming, drying, stimulation, and airway positioning. If the newborn remains apneic, gasping, or HR is below 100/min, PPV is the priority. If HR remains below 60/min despite effective ventilation, coordinated 3:1 compressions are indicated while continuing ventilation support.
key_teaching_points: The initial 30-second block (warm, dry, stimulate, position airway) determines whether the newborn needs PPV. Complete these steps before escalating — many newborns respond to stimulation alone.
PPV rate for newborns is 40–60 breaths/min. Use a pressure-limited neonatal BVM and verify chest rise. Adequate rate and seal are more important than high pressure.
The 3:1 compression-to-ventilation ratio for newborns (90 compressions + 30 breaths per minute) is different from pediatric and adult CPR. Ventilation is part of every cycle.
HR assessment drives every decision branch. Use auscultation over the sternum, not palpation of the cord or brachial pulse, for fastest reliable rate.
common_mistakes: Starting PPV without completing initial stimulation steps — many newborns respond to drying and airway positioning alone
Using adult or pediatric BVM — neonatal BVM with appropriate mask size is required to avoid barotrauma
Skipping HR reassessment after each intervention — the NRP algorithm is HR-driven; every branch point requires updated rate
Performing chest compressions without effective ventilation first — compressions are not indicated until PPV has been attempted and HR is still below 60/min
